00029 
00030 #ifndef _WCHAN_H_
00031 #define _WCHAN_H_
00032 
00033 /*
00034  * Wait channel.
00035  */
00036 
00037 
00038 struct wchan; /* Opaque */
00039 
00040 /*
00041  * Create a wait channel. Use NAME as a symbolic name for the channel.
00042  * NAME should be a string constant; if not, the caller is responsible
00043  * for freeing it after the wchan is destroyed.
00044  */
00045 struct wchan *wchan_create(const char *name);
00046 
00047 /*
00048  * Destroy a wait channel. Must be empty and unlocked.
00049  */
00050 void wchan_destroy(struct wchan *wc);
00051 
00052 /*
00053  * Return nonzero if there are no threads sleeping on the channel.
00054  * This is meant to be used only for diagnostic purposes.
00055  */
00056 bool wchan_isempty(struct wchan *wc);
00057 
00058 /*
00059  * Lock and unlock the wait channel.
00060  */
00061 void wchan_lock(struct wchan *wc);
00062 void wchan_unlock(struct wchan *wc);
00063 
00064 /*
00065  * Go to sleep on a wait channel. The current thread is suspended
00066  * until awakened by someone else, at which point this function
00067  * returns.
00068  *
00069  * The channel must be locked, and will have been *unlocked* upon
00070  * return.
00071  */
00072 void wchan_sleep(struct wchan *wc);
00073 
00074 /*
00075  * Wake up one thread, or all threads, sleeping on a wait channel.
00076  * The queue should not already be locked.
00077  *
00078  * The current implementation is FIFO but this is not promised by the
00079  * interface.
00080  */
00081 void wchan_wakeone(struct wchan *wc);
00082 void wchan_wakeall(struct wchan *wc);
00083 
00084 
00085 #endif /* _WCHAN_H_ */
